qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: HTTP Stream transport ignores maxMessageSize and crashes before auth on oversized request bodies
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same readRequestBody() logic in src/transports/http/server.ts that concatenates request chunks without enforcing maxMessageSize before auth on POST /mcp, which is the advisory's root cause.

selected_finding.title: HTTP Stream transport ignores maxMessageSize and crashes before auth on oversized request bodies

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only qualifying finding matches the advisory's readRequestBody()/maxMessageSize root cause, and the judge rerun from a copied workspace showed both activation of the missing size check and the resulting Node out-of-memory crash from an unauthenticated oversized POST.
evidence: The advisory and the finding both point to src/transports/http/server.ts readRequestBody() appending request chunks into a string without enforcing the configured maxMessageSize limit.
The finding's exploit path matches the advisory: POST /mcp reaches readRequestBody() and JSON.parse(body) before authentication or session rejection.

The judge rerun log at /Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_0a25d0cd/.judge/poc_runs/http_stream_oversized_body_dos/run.log shows [evohunt] oversized_probe_status=401 for a 2 MiB request even though the PoC configured maxMessageSize: 1024, demonstrating that the oversized body was accepted up to the auth path instead of being rejected for size.

The same run.log shows FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory, crash_request_result={"kind":"error","message":"read ECONNRESET"}, and EVOHUNT_HTTP_STREAM_DOS_CONFIRMED, which is the advisory-described denial-of-service consequence.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC accepted a 2 MiB POST body despite maxMessageSize: 1024 and returned 401 after the body was read, then a larger unauthenticated POST caused the server to terminate with a Node out-of-memory error and reset the client connection.
